news.title: ACB probes 2,600 illegal colonies in Haryana
news.para: Haryana's Vigilance Bureau is nearing the end of a major probe into approximately 2,600 illegal colonies. Officials from urban local bodies, town planning, and revenue departments face potential action for alleged collusion with developers. The investigation, mandated by the Punjab and Haryana High Court, aims to identify complicit government employees and could lead to large-scale demolitions.

news.title: Chandigarh administration rules out CLU, industrial plot conversion
news.para: Chandigarh administration has excluded long-pending demands for converting industrial plots to retail and permitting change of land use (CLU) in villages from its reform agenda. Officials cited a lack of contiguous land, high infrastructure costs, and master plan restrictions as reasons for these decisions, prioritizing industrial development instead.

news.title: About 2,000 notices issued over stilt area coverage violations in Gurugram, HC informed
news.para: Gurugram authorities have issued nearly 2,000 notices to property owners for illegally covering stilt areas. Around 500 restoration orders have also been passed. This information was shared with the Punjab and Haryana high court. The court is hearing a case regarding the 'stilt plus four floor policy' in Gurugram. The policy's implementation remains subject to the ongoing public interest litigation.

news.title: Bombay HC orders Maharashtra government to refund ₹3 lakh stamp duty to homebuyer
news.para: The Bombay High Court has ordered the state government to refund Rs 3 lakh in stamp duty to a homebuyer who mistakenly paid under the wrong head. Despite a slight delay in his refund application, the court ruled that the inadvertent error and unused stamps meant the government couldn't unjustly enrich itself.

news.title: NCLT approves ₹730 crore revival proposal for Rajesh Hotels
news.para: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved a revival plan for Rajesh Business & Leisure Hotels. A consortium of Rare Asset Reconstruction Company and Check-Inn Hotels will acquire the company. The plan involves a payment of ₹730 crore. This follows the company's admission into insolvency proceedings in April 2022. Secured creditors will receive a full settlement.

news.title: NCLAT ends insolvency proceedings against Embassy Development
news.para: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has overturned a lower court's decision to start insolvency proceedings against Embassy Development. The tribunal ruled that the case was barred by Section 10A of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. This section prevents insolvency proceedings for defaults occurring after March 25, 2020.

news.title: Sobha posts net profit of ₹193.40 crore in FY26
news.para: Sobha has announced 104.27 percent surge in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹193.40 crore. Total income also saw an increase of 29.33 percent. The fourth quarter of FY26 demonstrated robust growth with profit after tax rising by 124.80 percent.

news.title: SC orders DDA to refund ₹165 crore with 18 years of interest
news.para: The Supreme Court has ordered the Delhi Development Authority (DDA) to refund Rs 165 crore with interest, potentially totaling Rs 400 crore, to a company that purchased a commercial plot in Jasola. The DDA's acquisition of the land was deemed improper, and despite being given time to regularize it, the authority failed to do so, leading to the refund order.

news.title: Bombay HC clears Valor Estate subsidiary’s title over 205-acre Thane land
news.para: The Bombay High Court has dismissed the Union of India’s appeal claiming ownership of a prime 205-acre land parcel in Bhayandar. The ruling, which upholds a 2018 lower court decree, brings a definitive close to over 40 years of litigation between the Salt Department and Miraland Developers, a subsidiary of Valor Estate.

news.title: MahaRERA cracks down on errant builders; issues notices over 8,212 projects
news.para: MahaRERA is taking strict action against over 8,000 housing projects. These developers failed to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports on time. This includes many projects in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. MahaRERA has issued show-cause notices. Developers have 60 days to comply. Failure could lead to severe penalties, including project registration cancellation.

news.title: Time-bound system introduced to fix digital errors in property registration in Bihar
news.para: Citizens facing digital errors in land and property registration will now find relief. Bihar's prohibition, excise, and registration department has launched a new standard operating procedure. This system ensures corrections are made within a strict timeframe. Human and technical errors will be fixed efficiently. The department aims for a transparent and timely resolution of all discrepancies.

news.title: Ambuja Cements' net profit increases by 6.48% in FY26
news.para: Ambuja Cements, part of Adani Cement, announced a 6.48 percent rise in its net profit for FY26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹5,637.08 crore. Total income also saw a growth of 9.21 percent. The company recommended a dividend of ₹2 per equity share. Despite cost pressures, Ambuja Cements focused on consolidation and efficiency.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: 38 landlords booked for hiding tenant details
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad police have filed cases against 38 landlords for not reporting tenant details, a move linked to a crackdown on illegal immigrants, particularly Bangladeshis. This initiative aims to enhance security and prevent unauthorized stays. Property owners are urged to comply with tenant verification to avoid legal action, while citizens are encouraged to report suspicious individuals.

news.title: Godrej Properties' net profit rises 32.49% in FY26
news.para: Godrej Properties announced a significant 32.49 percent jump in its net profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹1,840.66 crore. Total income also saw a healthy increase of 20.72 percent. Strong business development additions and record operating cash flow are highlighted. The company plans further growth in the upcoming fiscal year.

news.title: NCLAT dismisses Vedanta's appeal against selection of Adani group's bid for Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: A company law appeals court has dismissed Vedanta's challenge to Gautam Adani's group winning bid for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd. The court found no merit in Vedanta's petitions. Adani's Rs 14,535 crore bid was preferred by creditors over Vedanta's plan. This ruling clears the way for Adani's takeover of Jaiprakash Associates, which owns India's only Formula One circuit.

news.title: Nearly half of building plan violation cases pending in Bhubaneswar civic body
news.para: Nearly half of the 9,500 building plan violation cases registered under the Odisha Development Authority Act, 1982, remain pending across Bhubaneswar's three zones.  These violations include construction without approval, deviations from sanctioned plans, and encroachments.  To expedite disposal, cases are now being redistributed among zonal offices, with the south-east zone having the highest backlog.

news.title: Haryana is India's third most groundwater-stressed state
news.para: Haryana is India's third most groundwater-stressed state, with 88 assessment units overexploited. The NGT has ordered a wider review, citing nationwide failures in preventing illegal extraction and ensuring groundwater recharge. Despite regulations, implementation gaps persist, prompting the NGT to form an expert committee to identify lapses and propose solutions.

news.title: Water crisis deepens Kazhakkoottam and Technopark in Thiruvananthapuram
news.para: A severe drinking water shortage is impacting Kazhakkoottam and Technopark, forcing residents and IT professionals to rely on tanker supply. Persistent issues with the ageing Aruvikkara supply network and frequent pipe bursts have disrupted distribution. Emergency measures include deploying water tankers and restoring local wells, while a Rs 68-crore pipeline project aims for a long-term solution within six months.

news.title: To generate revenue, Ludhiana civic body eyes sale of commercial properties
news.para: Facing financial strain, Ludhiana's civic body is set to auction its commercial properties, aiming to raise ₹50 crore in the fiscal year 2026-27.  Sixty-five shop-cum-offices in Shaheed Bhagat Singh Nagar and other prime locations are slated for sale. Officials are benchmarking rates against other development authorities to maximize revenue from these valuable assets.

news.title: Indiramma housing phase-II soon, bid to fix issues: Telangana minister
news.para: Telangana's Revenue and Housing Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy announced the imminent launch of the second phase of the Indiramma housing scheme. He highlighted the government's efforts to resolve land record issues and criticized the previous BRS government for failing to fulfill its housing promises. Reddy also reiterated the Congress government's commitment to farmers' welfare, citing crop procurement and loan waivers.
